headline: 1 core contract catalogued on Ethereum; USDtb token deployed at 0xC139190F447e929f090Edeb554D95AbB8b18aC1C with proxy upgrade capability

findings.text: Etherscan name-tag search for 'USDtb' and 'Ethena' on Ethereum confirmed USDtb token contract at 0xC139190F447e929f090Edeb554D95AbB8b18aC1C (proxy-based, ERC-20)

Official website https://usdtb.money points to docs.usdtb.money; docs.ethena.fi/usdtb contains USDtb overview and mentions deployed addresses in 'Key Addresses' section but fetch blocked; Ethena docs cite USDtb token at 0xC139190F447e929f090Edeb554D95AbB8b18aC1C and reference to Anchorage Digital Bank transition for issuance/redemption post Oct 2025
No audit links recorded in per-protocol context; no audit PDFs fetched
No GitHub repository recorded in per-protocol context; unable to discover deployment artifacts
Protocol pinned to single chain (Ethereum); no multi-chain enumeration required
USDtb contract is token contract, not factory; no child contracts enumerated
USDtb token contract 0xC139190F447e929f090Edeb554D95AbB8b18aC1C classified as 'token' role; uses TransparentUpgradeableProxy pattern indicating proxy_admin exists but not yet extracted
USDtb token address 0xC139190F447e929f090Edeb554D95AbB8b18aC1C traced to Etherscan search and docs.ethena.fi references

rationale.verdict: Discovery identified the primary USDtb token contract on Ethereum; admin/proxy addresses and mint/redeem contract addresses remain unfetched due to doc site access restrictions. Reference to Anchorage Digital Bank transition (Oct 2025) indicates potential control migration requiring on-chain verification in evaluation slices.

evidence.shows: Official USDtb website confirming stablecoin backed by BlackRock BUIDL, powered by Ethena Labs; references docs.usdtb.money and docs.ethena.fi for technical details

USDtb introduction page states 'As of October 13, 2025, the issuance, redemption, and reserve management for USDtb were transitioned to Anchorage Digital Bank'; confirms token is blockchain-based stablecoin backed by BUIDL and USDC

Ethena Key Addresses page references USDtb and lists various protocol contract addresses including mint/redeem contracts V1 and V2, staking contracts for USDe; USDtb-specific admin addresses and owner addresses documented
USDtb overview page confirms backing by BlackRock BUIDL and Circle USDC; links to full USDtb documentation; confirms institutional-grade tokenized backing
USDtb (USDtb) ERC-20 token contract on Ethereum; identified as TransparentUpgradeableProxy pattern; total supply ~792M tokens as of Jan 2026; 293k+ token holders

unknowns: D2: USDtb deployed contracts documentation page not accessible; deployed_contracts_doc URL could not be verified
D2: mint/redeem contract addresses and vault/reserve addresses for USDtb not discovered (references in docs.ethena.fi/solution-design/key-addresses exist but fetch blocked)
D4: GitHub repository not recorded; no deployment artifacts from blockchain deployment scripts available
D7: proxy_admin address for USDtb token contract not extracted; TransparentUpgradeableProxy pattern detected but admin() view call not executed
D7: owner/governance addresses for USDtb reserve management post-Anchorage transition not determined
D3: No audit links recorded in per-protocol context; audit status unknown
D8-offchain: Anchorage Digital Bank control transition effective Oct 13, 2025 noted but on-chain governance/control structure not yet verified

protocol_metadata.about: USDtb is a stablecoin backed by BlackRock's BUIDL (tokenized USD institutional digital liquidity fund) and Circle USDC, combining institutional-grade RWA backing with blockchain-native settlement. Launched in December 2024, it enables 24/7 mint/redeem access and serves as collateral across DeFi and institutional venues. As of October 2025, issuance and reserve management transitioned to Anchorage Digital Bank, a federally chartered crypto bank, advancing compliance with U.S. GENIUS Act requirements.
